content:
# Revelation Chapter 21 — The EMET Bible (Literal)
**Translator:** EMET | **Source:** Codex Sinaiticus (א), 4th century CE, pages 293–294 | **Source Image:** source-texts/greek-NT/REV_021_page_001.jpg, REV_021_page_002.jpg

1. And I saw a new heaven and a new earth; for the first heaven and the first earth passed away, and the sea is no more.

2. And the holy city, new Yerusalem, I saw coming down out of heaven from God, having been prepared as a bride adorned for her husband.

3. And I heard a great voice out of the throne saying, "Behold, the tabernacle of God [is] with men, and he will tabernacle with them, and they will be his peoples, and God himself will be with them,

4. and he will wipe away every tear from their eyes, and death will be no more; neither mourning nor crying nor pain will be any more, because the first things passed away."

5. And the one sitting upon the throne said, "Behold, I make all things new." And he says, "Write, because these words are faithful and true."

6. And he said to me, "They have come to pass. I am the Alpha and the Omega, the beginning and the end. To the one thirsting I will give out of the spring of the water of life freely.

7. The one overcoming will inherit these things, and I will be God to him and he will be a son to me.

8. But for the cowardly and unfaithful and abominable and murderers and fornicators and sorcerers and idolaters and all the liars, their part [is] in the lake burning with fire and sulfur, which is the second death."

9. And one of the seven angels having the seven bowls full of the seven last plagues came and spoke with me, saying, "Come, I will show you the bride, the wife of the Lamb."

10. And he carried me away in spirit upon a great and high mountain, and showed me the holy city Yerusalem coming down out of heaven from God,

11. having the glory of God. Her radiance [was] like a most precious stone, as a jasper stone, crystal-clear.

12. Having a wall great and high, having twelve gates, and at the gates twelve angels, and names having been inscribed which are [the names] of the twelve tribes of the sons of Israel:

13. from [the] east three gates, and from [the] north three gates, and from [the] south three gates, and from [the] west three gates.

14. And the wall of the city having twelve foundations, and upon them twelve names of the twelve apostles of the Lamb.

15. And the one speaking with me had a golden measuring-reed, so that he might measure the city and her gates and her wall.

16. And the city lies foursquare, and her length [is] as great as also the breadth. And he measured the city with the reed at twelve thousand stadia; the length and the breadth and the height of her are equal.

17. And he measured her wall [at] one hundred forty-four cubits, [by the] measure of a man, which is [that] of an angel.

18. And the building-material of her wall [was] jasper, and the city [was] pure gold, like pure glass.

19. The foundations of the wall of the city [were] adorned with every precious stone: the first foundation [was] jasper, the second sapphire, the third chalcedony, the fourth emerald,

20. the fifth sardonyx, the sixth sardius, the seventh chrysolite, the eighth beryl, the ninth topaz, the tenth chrysoprase, the eleventh hyacinth, the twelfth amethyst.

21. And the twelve gates [were] twelve pearls; each one of the gates was of one pearl. And the street of the city [was] pure gold, as transparent glass.

22. And a temple I did not see in her, for the Lord God the Almighty is her temple, and the Lamb.

23. And the city has no need of the sun nor of the moon that they should shine upon her, for the glory of God illuminated her, and her lamp [is] the Lamb.

24. And the nations will walk by means of her light, and the kings of the earth bring their glory into her.

25. And her gates will not be shut by day — for night will not be there —

26. and they will bring the glory and the honor of the nations into her.

27. And there will not enter into her anything common, nor [anyone] doing abomination and falsehood, except those having been written in the book of life of the Lamb.

---
## EMET's Textual Notes

1. **v.1 — "passed away":** apēlthan — aorist indicative, indicating a completed past action from the visionary's perspective, not a future event. Translated as simple past.

2. **v.2 — "new Yerusalem":** Sinaiticus reads Ierousalēm (the Hebraized form), not the Hellenized Hierosolyma. EMET renders this as "Yerusalem" to preserve the Semitic form present in the manuscript.

3. **v.3 — "peoples" (plural):** Sinaiticus reads laoi (peoples, plural) rather than the singular laos (people) found in many later manuscripts. This plural is theologically significant — the nations, not just Israel, become God's peoples.

4. **v.3 — "out of the throne":** ek tou thronou — Sinaiticus reads "out of the throne" rather than "out of heaven" (ek tou ouranou) as in some manuscripts. The voice comes from the throne itself.

5. **v.5 — Two speakers or one?:** The shift from "the one sitting upon the throne said" to "he says" (legei, present tense) is preserved. The abrupt tense change from aorist (eipen) to present (legei) is characteristic of the visionary style.

6. **v.6 — "They have come to pass":** gegonan — perfect tense, "they have become / have come to pass." Sinaiticus reads this plural form. Some manuscripts read gegona ("I have become"), which would yield "I am the Alpha and the Omega" as the same sentence. EMET follows the Sinaiticus reading.

7. **v.8 — "unfaithful":** apistois — Sinaiticus reads apistois (unfaithful/unbelieving) in this vice list. This is the standard Sinaiticus reading, distinct from some manuscripts that read a different term here.

8. **v.16 — "twelve thousand stadia":** dōdeka chiliadōn stadiōn — approximately 1,400 miles or 2,200 kilometers. Sinaiticus preserves this enormous measurement without qualification.

9. **v.17 — "measure of a man, which is of an angel":** metron anthrōpou, ho estin angelou — a striking parenthetical: the angel uses a human standard of measurement. Sinaiticus preserves this enigmatic note.

10. **v.22 — Word order:** naon ouk eidon en autē — "a temple not I-saw in her." The object (temple) is fronted for emphasis: it is the absence of a temple that is remarkable. EMET preserves this emphasis.

— EMET, AI Translator
